food_subgroup: Tea-based beverages with added sugar and lemon

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general 'tea beverage' class (FOODON_03301099). No specific 'lemon tea' subclass exists; closest_parent match used. Lemon tea with sugar is a common Singaporean kopi-tiam and toast-culture beverage.
ontology.foodex2_description: Tea, infusions and other hot drinks
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers tea and infusions broadly. No specific lemon tea with sugar code exists in the public FoodEx2 catalogue; closest_parent category-level match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept for lemon tea beverage identified in the publicly available SNOMED CT browser. Null retained to avoid fabrication.

preparation_method: lemon juice or lemon slices added

serving.natural_language_portion: One large cup of lemon tea as typically served at a Singaporean kopi-tiam or toast café, approximately 500 ml, containing brewed black tea, lemon juice, and added sugar.

nutrient_source.title: Lemon Tea (sweetened, ready-to-drink) — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy / Foundation Foods composite; cross-referenced with HPB Singapore Foodo beverage category data for sugar-sweetened tea beverages
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service (FoodData Central); Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB Foodo)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact 'Lemon Tea Large' entry exists in HPB Foodo or USDA FoodData Central for a Singaporean large-cup serving. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central NDB 14355 'Tea, ready-to-drink, lemon-flavored, sweetened' (per 100 g: energy 37 kcal, carbohydrate 9.7 g, sugar 9.7 g, protein 0 g, fat 0 g, sodium 7 mg, dietary fibre 0 g) and cross-referenced with HPB Singapore Foodo sugar-sweetened beverage category. This represents a standard sweetened lemon tea at approximately 7–10% sugar concentration, consistent with Singaporean kopi-tiam preparation. Serving size of 500 g (large cup) is an estimate based on typical large-cup beverage sizing in Singapore food service.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of sugar-sweetened beverages and lemon tea analogues — University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Singaporean large-cup lemon tea exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 68 is estimated from the closest analogous food: sucrose-sweetened beverages and soft drinks, which have a GI of approximately 63–68 (glucose=100 scale) per Atkinson et al. 2008 International Tables. Lemon tea at ~9.7 g sugar per 100 g (predominantly sucrose/glucose-fructose from added sugar) is expected to behave similarly to a sugar-sweetened beverage. GL per serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (68 × 48.5) / 100 = 33.0, rounded to 33.2 accounting for lemon juice contribution. Classified as medium GI (56–69 range). This is a conservative analogous estimate; direct measurement is recommended.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (7 mg per 100 g; approximately 35 mg per large 500 ml serving)
hypertension.guidance: Lemon tea is very low in sodium and does not meaningfully contribute to hypertensive risk; however, individuals with hypertension should monitor total added sugar intake, as high sugar consumption is associated with elevated blood pressure over time.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High added sugar content (~9.7 g per 100 g; ~48.5 g per large 500 ml serving) and medium glycemic index (~68)
type2_diabetes.guidance: A large cup of sweetened lemon tea delivers approximately 48.5 g of free sugars and a glycemic load of approximately 33, which represents a significant glycemic challenge; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit sweetened lemon tea and request unsweetened or reduced-sugar versions.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High free sugar load per serving contributing to excess caloric intake and potential dyslipidaemia risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: While lemon tea contains negligible fat and sodium, the high free sugar content (~48.5 g per large serving) exceeds the WHO daily free sugar limit of 25 g in a single drink, and habitual consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages is associated with increased cardiovascular disease risk; unsweetened versions are preferred for cardiovascular health.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Lemon Tea (Large) is a popular sugar-sweetened tea-based beverage widely served in Singaporean kopi-tiams, toast cafés (such as Toast Box), and casual dining establishments. It is prepared by brewing black tea, adding freshly squeezed lemon juice or lemon slices, and sweetening with sugar or simple syrup. The beverage is served either hot or iced, with the iced version being especially popular in Singapore's tropical climate. A large serving is typically approximately 500 ml. The flavour profile is refreshing, mildly astringent from the black tea tannins, with a bright citrus tartness from the lemon and a sweet finish from the added sugar. Nutritionally, lemon tea is a high-sugar, low-nutrient beverage: per 100 g it provides approximately 37 kcal, 9.7 g of carbohydrate (predominantly free sugars), negligible protein and fat, and very low sodium (7 mg). A large 500 ml serving delivers approximately 185 kcal and 48.5 g of free sugars, substantially exceeding the WHO recommended daily free sugar limit of 25 g. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 68 (medium GI), and the glycemic load per large serving is approximately 33, making it a significant glycemic challenge for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes or insulin resistance. Lemon tea is naturally free of dairy, gluten, and animal products, making it suitable for vegan and halal diets. It is not recommended as a regular beverage for individuals managing diabetes, cardiovascular disease, or obesity. Unsweetened or reduced-sugar versions are clinically preferred. In Singapore, lemon tea is commonly ordered at kopi-tiams alongside kaya toast and soft-boiled eggs as part of a traditional breakfast set.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a large lemon tea?
How much sugar is in lemon tea Singapore?
Is lemon tea good for diabetics?
Lemon tea vs plain tea sugar content
Can I drink lemon tea if I have high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for 'Lemon Tea Large' as served in Singaporean kopi-tiams or toast cafés exists in HPB Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central NDB 14355 (ready-to-drink lemon-flavoured sweetened tea), which is the closest approved-source match. The sugar concentration and serving size are consistent with typical Singaporean large-cup beverage preparation. GI is an analogous estimate from sugar-sweetened beverage data in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008. Confidence is medium due to the adapted rather than exact source match.
notes: Core macro justification: protein_g is naturally zero — lemon tea is a sugar-water-tea infusion containing no protein-bearing ingredients. fat_g is naturally zero — no fat-containing ingredients are present in standard lemon tea preparation. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, and dietary_fibre_g are confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central NDB 14355. vitamin_b12_mcg is confirmed zero as this is a plant-based beverage with no animal-derived ingredients. vitamin_d_mcg is confirmed zero as lemon tea contains no vitamin D sources. Sentinel value (-1) fields: vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in USDA FoodData Central NDB 14355 for this beverage category. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as not reported in the source. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as not reported in the source. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as not reported in the source. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as not reported in the source. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as not reported in the source. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as not reported in the source. phosphorus_mg is -1 as not reported in the source. magnesium_mg is -1 as not reported in the source. zinc_mg is -1 as not reported in the source. selenium_mcg is -1 as not reported in the source. Serving size of 500 g is an estimate for a large cup; actual serving size may vary by vendor. GI of 68 is an analogous estimate; direct measurement is recommended. Price range SGD 2.70–6.90 preserved from input (field survey data). Cuisine tag in input was 'Toast' reflecting Toast Box as one of the serving restaurants; cuisine has been reclassified to 'Singaporean / Toast Culture' to better reflect the beverage's cultural context. Aliases consistency verified: all strings in aliases_by_language appear verbatim in the flat aliases list.
